title: Rain alert in Rajasthan tomorrow, heatwave today: Sri Ganganagar hottest in country for second straight day... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Rain alert in Rajasthan tomorrow, heatwave today:Sri Ganganagar hottest in country for second straight day; mercury crosses 46°C
In Rajasthan, an alert for storms, rain, and heatwave has been issued for Wednesday as well. A yellow alert has been issued for rain in 4 districts and for heatwave in 6 districts. After this, rainfall will intensify from June 11.
Earlier on Tuesday, Sri Ganganagar remained the hottest place in the country for the second consecutive day. Here, the maximum temperature went above 46 degrees Celsius.
In Churu, Bikaner, Phalodi, and Jaisalmer too, the maximum temperature was recorded between 43 to 45 degrees. Meanwhile, in areas bordering Haryana and Delhi NCR, the weather changed in the afternoon. Dust storms occurred and light to moderate rain occurred in many areas.
According to the Meteorological Department, a western disturbance will become active in the state from June 11. Due to this, there is an orange and yellow alert for dust storms, rain, and hailstorms.
Updates Related to Heat and Rain
Weather of the Last 24 Hours: The maximum temperature in Sriganganagar was recorded at 46.4 degrees Celsius on Tuesday. Apart from Sriganganagar, temperatures were recorded at 44.6 in Churu, 43.8 in Phalodi, 43.6 in Bikaner and Jaisalmer, and 44.5 degrees Celsius in Pilani. All these areas experienced the most severe impact of the heatwave on Tuesday.
Stormy Rain in Alwar, Hanumangarh: After intense sunshine and heat during the day, weather changed in districts bordering Haryana, Delhi NCR and some of their areas. Clouds gathered in the afternoon in areas of Hanumangarh, Alwar, Jhunjhunu, Bharatpur and strong dusty winds blew in many areas. Rain occurred along with storms in Hanumangarh and Alwar.
What's Next:With another new western disturbance becoming active from June 11, activities of storms and rain are likely to increase in many parts of the state. During June 11 to 14, storms with strong thundering (50-60 Kmph) and light to moderate rain at some places are likely in some parts of Bikaner, Jaipur, Ajmer, Bharatpur divisions.

Sikar: The phase of intense heat has started once again in the district. On Tuesday, the maximum temperature crossed 40 degrees. The maximum temperature in Sikar was recorded at 41 degrees. Earlier, the minimum temperature here was recorded at 39.8 degrees on Monday.
